questions.question: Tulika Maan and Sushila Likmabam, who clinched medals for India, play which sports?
questions.explanation: Tulika Maan clinched a silver medal for India in Judo at the Commonwealth Games 2022 in the 78 kg category. Earlier, Sushila Likmabam got a silver medal in the Women’s 48kg category, and Vijay Kumar Yadav won a bronze medal in the Men’s 60kg category in Judo.

questions.question: Who has been selected as the 14th Vice President of India?
questions.explanation: Jagdeep Dhankhar has won India’s Vice Presidential election 2022, defeating Opposition candidate Margaret Alva. Dhankhar will be India’s 14th Vice President, taking over from the incumbent Vice President M Venkaiah Naidu. He has served as a Minister of state for Parliamentary Affairs in the Janata Dal government. He became the Governor of West Bengal in July 2019.

questions.question: What is India’s new target year to eliminate Kala-azar?
questions.explanation: The Government has set the target to eliminate Kala-azar by 2023 from the country. In a written reply in Lok Sabha given by the Union Health Minister, out of 633 Kala-azar endemic blocks, 625 blocks have achieved the elimination target last year. The World Health Organisation (WHO) Neglected Tropical Diseases Road map has set the target for Kala-azar elimination as 2030.

questions.question: Which institution released the ‘Prevention of insider trading (PIT) regulations’?
questions.explanation: Market regulator SEBI has released a new framework that will prevent company insiders from dealing in shares during the closure of the trading window. Under the Prevention of insider trading (PIT) regulations, the trading window is closed when the compliance officer determines that a designated person or class of designated persons possess unpublished price-sensitive information.

questions.question: What is the position of India in the 2022 Commonwealth Games?
questions.explanation: India finished 4th on the podium at the Commonwealth Games 2022 with a medal tally of 61, including 22 Gold, 16 Silver, and 23 Bronze. India is after Australia 178 (67 Gold), England 176 (57 Gold), Canada 92 (26 Gold). The Birmingham Games is India’s fourth-best performance at the CWG. Wrestlers contributed the most to the medal tally of India with all 12 of them clinching medals. Sharath Kamal, the 40-year-old table tennis star, was the most successful Indian athlete with 4 medals, including 3 Gold.

questions.question: For the first time, a U.S. Navy Ship arrived in India for carrying out repairs and services, at which shipyard?
questions.explanation: For the first time, a U.S. Navy Ship arrived in India for carrying out repairs and services at Larsen & Toubro’s (L&T) Shipyard at Kattupalli, Chennai. The ship will undergo maintenance activities at the Shipyard for 11 days. This is seen as a new dimension to the Indo-U.S. strategic partnership.

questions.question: Who was appointed as the first woman chief of the Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR)?
questions.explanation: Dr N Kalaiselvi has become the first woman Director General of the Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR) The Energy researcher will be the Secretary to the Department of Scientific and Industrial Research. She is the only fourth woman scientist to occupy the position of a Secretary of a scientific department under the central government.

questions.question: When is the ‘World Bio-fuel Day’ celebrated?
questions.explanation: ‘World Bio-fuel Day’ is celebrated annually on August 10 to highlight the importance of bio-fuels. On this date in 1893 that German inventor Sir Rudolf Diesel successfully operated his diesel engine on peanut oil. Bio-fuels are produced from animal waste, algae, industrial and agricultural waste. Unlike fossil fuels, they are produced in a short amount of time and are liquid or gaseous. They are environment friendly, renewable and biodegradable. Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas (MoP&NG) and the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) started celebrating World Biofuel Day after 2015.

questions.question: India is set to implement joint research projects with which country through Technology Innovation Hubs (TIH)?
questions.explanation: Experts from the India and United States interacted to plan joint research projects that would be implemented through the Technology Innovation Hubs (TIH) in a work shop. The DST-NSF Joint Research and Development Projects Kick-off Workshop was organised by IIT Delhi in association with Department of Science and Technology.

questions.question: Which Indian state launched ‘Nethanna Bima’ scheme for benefitting weavers?
questions.explanation: Telangana Chief Minister K Chandrashekhar Rao launched the Nethanna Bima scheme for the weavers on the occasion of National Handloom Day. This scheme will provide ₹5 lakh insurance cover to the weavers’ families in case of the unfortunate demise of an eligible beneficiary. Telangana government has partnered with Life Insurance Corporation (LIC) of India for the ‘Nethanna Beema’ scheme.
